name: Black Sabbath
bio: Black Sabbath has a durable heavy metal catalog that continues to attract listeners through streaming, playlists, and long-tail discovery.
notable_for: Black Sabbath remains closely associated with Paranoid and Iron Man, which continue to anchor catalog attention.
career_highlight: Songs like Paranoid and Iron Man still help define the catalog's long-tail earnings profile.
revenue_drivers: Classic catalog streaming keeps major songs active well beyond the original release cycle.
Playlist longevity and generational rediscovery support steady long-tail listening.
Film, television, sports, and trailer usage can reactivate demand for familiar recordings.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps Black Sabbath's current headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: Black Sabbath's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
